body.title: agent_datacenter: verify Igor→Claude and Igor→Inference are identical comms:// call pattern
body.result: comms:// unification validated — identical call pattern for igor→inference and igor→claude in contract tests
body.description:
Verify the comms:// unification invariant: Igor talks to Claude and Igor talks to Inference using the identical call pattern to different addresses. The caller is topology-blind.

This is the architectural invariant the bus is designed to provide. If this test fails, the design is broken.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/tests/test_comms_unification.py:
  def test_igor_to_claude_and_inference_identical_pattern():
    # Build two envelopes: one to CC.0, one to inference
    # Send both using Router.send(address, envelope) — same call pattern
    # Verify: Router.send() doesn't inspect or branch on destination
    # Verify: both deliveries succeed (envelope in CC.0 mailbox; envelope in inference mailbox)
    # Verify: the calling code is identical — no if/else on destination
  def test_router_does_not_special_case_any_address():
    # Inspect Router.send() — assert no isinstance checks or address-specific branches
    # If Router.send() has branching logic for 'CC.0' vs 'inference', this test should catch it

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/tests/test_comms_unification.py

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: test docstrings explain the 'topology-blind caller' invariant

Scope boundary:
IN: comms unification test — same call pattern for CC and inference
OUT: YGM pipeline (different test), performance comparison of routes

Test plan:
- Both tests pass with stub devices for CC and inference
- If Router.send() gains a special case, this test breaks and surfaces it
